mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which correlates with rising corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative SPX 20-day returns in >50% of early-cycle observations, or out-of-sample Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the joint signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative SPX 60-day returns in >55% of monthly observations, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate dropping below 1.0% signals severe economic deceleration and recession probability, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%, or the threshold trigger produces no significant alpha (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial output and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction that reduces corporate cash flows and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative SPX 20-day returns in >50% of historical cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
